# docs/project-scope.md
# Generated by /team-brief discovery step. Update manually if project scope changes.
# Read by: /team-design (Step 1), /team-plan (Step 1), /team-build (optional worktree), /team-qa (routing), /team-tdd (domain rows).
# generated_at: 2026-04-29

domains:
  - agentic-systems   # primary — orchestrator for per-agent-group containers, MCP tools, multi-agent routing
  - llm-engineering   # secondary — Anthropic Agent SDK, prompt assembly, recall context injection

primary_language: typescript
framework: node-host-bun-container   # Node host process; per-agent-group containers run Bun
test_framework: vitest                # host (Node); container uses bun:test

relevant_global_skills:
  - agentic-systems
  - llm-engineering

quality_gates:
  - "host typecheck via tsc passes; container typecheck via bun run typecheck passes"
  - "vitest suite passes on host; bun:test passes inside container/agent-runner"
  - "no destructive operations on production session DBs without backup"
  - "supply-chain hygiene: pnpm minimumReleaseAge: 4320 (3 days) preserved; no minimumReleaseAgeExclude additions without operator approval"

security_surface:
  - "credential injection via OneCLI proxy (containers must not see raw API keys)"
  - "cross-tenant store isolation (per-group MNEMON_STORE + per-store mount narrowing)"
  - "agent-issued shell or filesystem actions: must respect mount allowlist + sender allowlist"
  - "prompt injection via user messages, sources files, recall results, or wiki content"
  - "secrets in source ingestion: extractor must refuse to store anything credential-like"

description: >
  NanoClaw v2 is a personal AI assistant orchestrator. The host is a single Node process
  that manages per-agent-group containers (Bun runtime, Anthropic Agent SDK). Communication
  between host and containers happens through two SQLite files per session (inbound.db /
  outbound.db) — no IPC, no stdin piping. Each agent group has its own filesystem,
  CLAUDE.md, skills, and container config. Channel adapters (Slack, Discord, Telegram,
  WhatsApp, etc.) install as skill branches that copy code into standard paths.

  Memory layer (this rebuild) is being rearchitected to replicate Vivian Balakrishnan's
  v1 NanoClaw architecture from gist a7d4eec3833baee4971a0ee54b08f322 — three-layer
  pipeline (raw sources → mnemon graph → wiki pages), three operations (extract,
  synthesise, recall), with auto-recall as system-reminder push (not agent-driven pull)
  and curated source ingestion (not chat-stream extraction).
